proxy.last_saved_proxy_configuration:
name: coolify-proxy
networks:
  coolify:
    external: true
services:
  traefik:
    container_name: coolify-proxy
    image: 'traefik:v3.6'
    restart: unless-stopped
    extra_hosts:
      - 'host.docker.internal:host-gateway'
    networks:
      - coolify
    ports:
      - '80:80'
      - '443:443'
      - '443:443/udp'
      - '8080:8080'
    healthcheck:
      test: 'wget -qO- http://localhost:80/ping || exit 1'
      interval: 4s
      timeout: 2s
      retries: 5
    volumes:
      - '/var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock:ro'
      - '/data/coolify/proxy/:/traefik'
    command:
      - '--ping=true'
      - '--ping.entrypoint=http'
      - '--api.dashboard=true'
      - '--entrypoints.http.address=:80'
      - '--entrypoints.https.address=:443'
      - '--entrypoints.http.http.encodequerysemicolons=true'
      - '--entryPoints.http.http2.maxConcurrentStreams=250'
      - '--entrypoints.https.http.encodequerysemicolons=true'
      - '--entryPoints.https.http2.maxConcurrentStreams=250'
      - '--entrypoints.https.http3'
      - '--providers.file.directory=/traefik/dynamic/'
      - '--providers.file.watch=true'
      - '--certificatesresolvers.letsencrypt.acme.httpchallenge=true'
      - '--certificatesresolvers.letsencrypt.acme.httpchallenge.entrypoint=http'
      - '--certificatesresolvers.letsencrypt.acme.storage=/traefik/acme.json'
      - '--api.insecure=false'
      - '--providers.docker=true'
      - '--providers.docker.exposedbydefault=false'
    labels:
      - traefik.enable=true
      - traefik.http.routers.traefik.entrypoints=http
      - traefik.http.routers.traefik.service=api@internal
      - traefik.http.services.traefik.loadbalancer.server.port=8080
      - coolify.managed=true
      - coolify.proxy=true

commit_message:
Fix: users with no page get stuck with broken dashboard links

Root cause of "signup didn't give me a username" + Embed link showing
naked/root URL + View page going to homepage: Dashboard rendered for
ANY logged-in user regardless of whether they'd actually finished
onboarding (created a page/username). If someone dropped off mid-wizard
or otherwise reached /dashboard without a page, every username-dependent
link silently fell back to the homepage instead of erroring or guiding
them back to finish setup.

Fixes:
- Dashboard.jsx: redirect to /onboarding if the auth user has no
  username, instead of rendering a half-broken page.
- auth.js: signup/login responses now include `username` (previously
  only /api/auth/me did), so the guard above has accurate data
  immediately instead of one stale render before a refresh reconciled it.
- Onboarding.jsx: refresh the auth user after completing onboarding,
  before navigating to /dashboard -- without this the new Dashboard
  guard would immediately bounce a just-onboarded user right back into
  the wizard (stale `username: null` from the original signup/login
  response never got updated client-side).

Also includes the python3-setuptools Docker native-build fix (same
issue as the rest of the suite -- see other repos' matching commits).
